title: Bridge Essay: Touch neurons underlying dopaminergic pleasurable touch and sexual receptivity.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Touch neurons underlying dopaminergic pleasurable touch and sexual receptivity.** — Cell. PMID: 36693373. DOI: 10.1016/j.cell.2022.12.034. Pleasurable touch is paramount during social behavior, including sexual encounters. However, the identity and precise role of sensory neurons that transduce sexual touch remain unknown. Apopulation of sensory neurons labeled by developmental expression of the Gprotein-coupled receptor Mrgprb4 detects mechanical stimulation in mice. Here, we study the social relevance of Mrgprb4-lineage neurons and reveal that these neurons are required for sexual receptivity and sufficient to induce dopamine r
